title: croton panduriformis
intro: Croton panduriformis, also known as the Fingerroot or Chinese Fingerroot, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in China, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its roots and rhizomes possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. It has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Croton panduriformis include teas, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
